title: Hybrid field of view target system
abstract: A robotic system (402) automatically aligns, and/or tests alignment of, a lens (704, 804) to a digital camera (706, 806) or other workpiece (700, 800). The system (402) includes an optical target (106), an intermediate lens (318) and a plurality of collimators (400) peripheral to the intermediate lens (318) to accommodate a wide range of fields of view (702, 802) of the workpieces (700, 800), without requiring changes in equipment hardware. When manufacturing or testing a workpiece (700, 800) with a relatively narrow field of view (702), the entire field of view (702) of the workpiece (700) can be filled with a view of the target (106) through the intermediate lens (318), and the collimators (400) need not be used. However, when manufacturing or testing a camera having a relatively large field of view (802), the intermediate lens (318) is used to fill a central portion (1102) of the field of view (802) with an image of the target (106), and the collimators (400) are used to fill a remaining portion of the field of view with images (1200-1204) of reticles (912).
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A system (402) for automatically aligning, and/or testing alignment of, a workpiece (700) having a workpiece optical axis (902), wherein during a first time period the workpiece comprises a first lens (704) and a first image sensor (706) collectively having a first field of view (702), and during a second time period the workpiece (800) comprises a second lens (804) and a second image sensor (806) collectively having a second field of view (802) wider than the first field of view (702), the system (402) comprising: a processor (406); a target (106) configured to translate substantially along the respective workpiece optical axis (902), in response to a first command from the processor, to a position specified by the processor (406), the processor (406) being configured to thereby selectively adjust optical spacing between the target (106) and the workpiece (700 or 800); an intermediate lens (318) disposed optically between the target (106) and the workpiece (700 or 800) and configured to form: during the first time period, a first image (1000) of the target (106) on the first image sensor (706), via a sequence of the intermediate lens (318) and the first lens (704), the first image (1000) filling a first fraction of the first image sensor (706); and during the second time period, a second image (1104) of the target (106) on a central portion (1102) of the second image sensor (806), via a sequence of the intermediate lens (318) and the second lens (804), the second image (1104) filling a second fraction of the second image sensor (806) smaller than the first fraction; a plurality of collimators (400) disposed peripheral to the intermediate lens (318), each collimator having a respective reticle (912) and being configured to project, during the second time period, a respective image (1200-1204) of the reticle (912), through the second lens (804), on the second image sensor (806), without the image (1200-1204) of the reticle (912) passing through the intermediate lens (318), such that the image (1200-1204) of the reticle (912) extends outside an outer boundary (1206) of the second image (1104) of the target (106) on the second image sensor (806); and 

 a robot (100) configured to grasp and, in response to a second command (408) from the processor (406), scan at least a portion of the workpiece (700 or 800) along a trajectory; wherein the processor is configured to analyze signals (410) acquired from the workpiece (700 or 800) while the workpiece (700 or 800) is scanned along the trajectory and thereby quantitatively assess a degree of optical alignment of the workpiece (700 or 800). 2. A system according to claim 1, wherein focal length of each collimator of the plurality of collimators (400) is adjustable, and each collimator is configured to adjust its focal length in response to a third command from the processor. 3. A system according to claim 1, wherein the plurality of collimators (400) comprises at least four collimators. 4. A system according to claim 1, wherein the first field of view (702) is less than about 60Â°. 5. A system according to claim 1, wherein the second field of view (802) is greater than about 60Â°. 6. A system according to claim 1, wherein each collimator of the plurality of collimators (400) is oriented toward an entrance pupil (920) center of the workpiece (700 or 800), location of the entrance pupil (920) center being based at least in part on a field of view of interest. 7. A system according to claim 1, further comprising, for each collimator, a respective folding optical element (1300) configured to redirect the respective image of the reticle (912) toward an entrance pupil (920) center of the workpiece (700 or 800), location of the entrance pupil (920) center being based at least in part on a field of view of interest. 8. A system according to claim 7, wherein an angle (1304) of each respective folding optical element (1300) is adjustable. 9. A system according to claim 7, wherein an optical axis of each collimator of the plurality of collimators (400) is oriented parallel to the workpiece optical axis (902). 10. A system according to claim 1, wherein an optical axis of each collimator of the plurality of collimators (400) is adjustable, relative to the workpiece optical axis (902). 11. A system according to claim 1, wherein the plurality of collimators (400) is attached via a kinematic mount. 12. A system according to claim 1, further comprising an optical stage (404) configured to releasably receive the plurality of collimators (404), such that number and/or disposition of collimators in the plurality of collimators (400) is adjustable. 13. A system according to claim 1, wherein each collimator of the plurality of collimators (400) is individually removable. 14. A system according to claim 1, wherein at least some of the respective reticles (912) of the plurality of collimators (400) are different from other of the respective reticles (912) of the plurality of collimators (400). 15. A system according to claim 1, wherein at least some collimators of the plurality of collimators (400) are configured to project respective images (1200-1204) of the respective reticles (912) with different wavelengths of light from other of the collimators of the plurality of collimators (400). 16. A system according to claim 1, wherein wavelength of light from the plurality of collimators (400) is adjustable. 17. A system according to claim 1, wherein color and/or intensity of the images (1200-1204) of the respective reticles (912) is adjustable. 18. A system according to claim 1, wherein at least some collimators of the plurality of collimators (400) are configured to project respective images (1200-1204) of the respective reticles (912) at different angles, relative to the workpiece optical axis (902), from other of the collimators of the plurality of collimators (400). 19. A system according to claim 1, wherein the plurality of collimators (400) is configured to translate along the respective workpiece optical axis (902), in response to a fourth command from the processor (406), to a position specified by the processor (406), the processor (406) being configured to thereby selectively adjust optical spacing between the plurality of collimators (400) and the workpiece (700 or 800) in coordination with the optical spacing between the target (106) and the workpiece (700 or 800). 20. A system according to claim 1, wherein the plurality of collimators (400) is immobile, relative to the intermediate lens (318), along the workpiece optical axis (902). 21. A system according to claim 1, wherein the plurality of collimators (400) is mobile, relative to the intermediate lens (318), along the workpiece optical axis (902). 22. A system according to claim 1, wherein the processor (406) is configured to automatically control projection by the plurality of collimators (400) of the respective images (1200-1204) of the respective reticles (912), based at least in part on magnitude of the field of view (702 or 802) of a current workpiece (700 or 800). 23. A non-transitory computer-readable medium encoded with instructions that, when executed by a processor (406), establish processes for performing a computer-implemented method (1400) of automatically aligning, and/or testing alignment of, a workpiece (700 or 800) having a workpiece optical axis (902), wherein during a first time period the workpiece (700) comprises a first lens (704) and a first image sensor (706) collectively having a first field of view (702), and during a second time period the workpiece (800) comprises a second lens (804) and a second image sensor (806) collectively having a second field of view (802) wider than the first field of view (702), the processes comprising: a process (1402) configured to translate a target (106) substantially along the respective workpiece optical axis (902), in response to a first command from the processor (406), to a position specified by the processor (406), the processor (406) being configured to thereby selectively adjust optical spacing between the target (106) and the workpiece (700 or 800) and, during the first time period, form (1404) a first image (1000) of the target (106) on the first image sensor (706), via a sequence of an intermediate lens (318), disposed optically between the target (106) and the workpiece (700), and the first lens (704), the first image (1000) filling a first fraction of the first image sensor (706), and during the second time period, form (1406) a second image (1004) of the target (106) on a central portion (1102) of the second image sensor (806), via a sequence of the intermediate lens (318) and the second lens (804), the second image (1004) filling a fraction of the second image sensor (806) smaller than the first fraction; a process (1410) configured to control a plurality of collimators (400) disposed peripheral to the intermediate lens (318), each collimator having a respective reticle (912) and being configured to project (914), during the second time period, a respective image (1200-1204) of the reticle (912), through the second lens (804), on the second image sensor (806), without the image (1200-1204) of the reticle (912) passing through the intermediate lens (318), such that the image (1200-1204) of the reticle (912) extends outside an outer boundary (1206) of the second image (1104) of the target (106) on the second image sensor (806); a process (1412) configured to control a robot (100) configured to grasp and, in response to a second command (408) from the processor (406), scan at least a portion of the workpiece (700 or 800) along a trajectory; and 

 a process (1414) configured to analyze signals (410) acquired from the workpiece (700 or 800) while the workpiece (700 or 800) is scanned along the trajectory and thereby quantitatively assess a degree of optical alignment of the workpiece (700 or 800).
